purpose: Investigate why OG ROM does not respond to SELECT for form toggling while remake does

test1_single_frame_select.description: Single frame SELECT press, no other buttons

test4_debounce_30f_then_select.description: Full debounce (30 frames all keys released) then single SELECT

test5_walk_stop_select.description: Walk RIGHT 60 frames, stop 15 frames, then SELECT

test8_per_frame_monitor.description: Per-frame FFBE monitoring during 5-frame SELECT hold
test8_per_frame_monitor.detail: FFBE=0 on every single frame, DD09=0 (input not blocked)

test9_direct_write.description: Direct memory write to FFBE (bypass game logic)
test9_direct_write.detail: Game accepts and retains the direct write - does not overwrite FFBE

test1_single_frame_select.description: Single frame SELECT press, no other buttons

test2_hold_select_10f.detail: Edge-triggered: toggles on press frame only, not during hold

test4_debounce_30f_then_select.description: Full debounce (30 frames all keys released) then single SELECT

test5_walk_stop_select.description: Walk RIGHT 60 frames, stop 15 frames, then SELECT

test8_per_frame_monitor.description: Per-frame FFBE monitoring during 5-frame SELECT hold
test8_per_frame_monitor.detail: Toggle occurs exactly 1 frame after addKey(2), edge-triggered

test9_direct_write.description: Direct memory write to FFBE (bypass game logic)
test9_direct_write.detail: Remake overwrites FFBE from internal game state (player.form). Direct write does not persist.

0x11EC_bank0.context: Sets FFBE=1 (Dragon form) as part of item/powerup use routine
0x11EC_bank0.trigger: FFE1 state machine: FFE1==1 triggers Dragon form
0x11EC_bank0.surrounding_code: Sets FFE1=2, FFBE=1, FFE5=5, calls 0x0B80, jumps to 0x4944

0x1211_bank0.context: Sets FFBE=0 (Witch form) as part of item/powerup use routine
0x1211_bank0.trigger: FFE1 state machine: FFE1==2 triggers Witch form restore
0x1211_bank0.surrounding_code: XOR A -> FFBE=0, FFE1=0, FFE5=5, calls 0x0B80, jumps to 0x4944

0x40E3_bank1.context: Game initialization/reset: clears all game state including FFBE
0x40E3_bank1.surrounding_code: Part of massive state clear: FFC0, FFE1, FFEB, FFBE, FFFC, FFC1

input_system.joypad_mask: FF96 - button mask (0xFF = all buttons allowed)
input_system.joypad_edge: FF94 = (FF93 XOR FF95) AND FF93 AND FF96 (newly-pressed, masked)
input_system.select_handler: 0x0A6C: checks FF94 AND 0x04, calls item menu at 0x1D5D

select_button_behavior.og_game: SELECT opens item/status menu (function 0x1D5D). Menu shows inventory at DCBD+. Form change requires selecting a transformation item from the menu. SELECT does NOT directly toggle FFBE.
select_button_behavior.remake: SELECT directly toggles FFBE (player.form ^= 1) via edge detection in player_update(). This is a remake-specific simplification, NOT faithful to the original game mechanics.

menu_function_0x1D5D.behavior: Opens Window layer (LCDC bit 5), enters button dispatch loop
menu_function_0x1D5D.a_button: Use selected item (may trigger form change via FFE1 state machine)
menu_function_0x1D5D.b_button: Close menu (B or SELECT closes, RES bit 5 on LCDC)
menu_function_0x1D5D.ffda_gate: FFDA controls whether setup code runs before menu, but menu always called

form_change_mechanism.description: Form changes are item-based, not button-based in the OG game
form_change_mechanism.state_machine: FFE1 acts as a form-change trigger: 0=none, 1=to-Dragon, 2=to-Witch
form_change_mechanism.code_at_0x11C3: Checks FFE1: if 1, sets FFBE=1+FFE1=2; if 2, sets FFBE=0+FFE1=0
form_change_mechanism.inventory: DCBD-DCDA region stores items. DCDB is inventory cursor. DCDD is HP.
form_change_mechanism.note: At game start, inventory is empty (all zeros at DCBD+), so form toggle is impossible until transformation item is picked up

root_cause.summary: The OG game uses SELECT to open an ITEM MENU, not to directly toggle form. Form changes are item-based (require a transformation powerup item from inventory). The remake incorrectly implements SELECT as a direct form toggle.
root_cause.og_behavior: SELECT opens item/inventory menu (Window layer). Player must have a transformation item and use it from the menu to change form. Without items in inventory, form cannot be changed.
root_cause.remake_behavior: SELECT directly toggles player.form via XOR (player.c line 49: player.form ^= 1). This is edge-triggered and works immediately.
root_cause.recommendation: The remake should implement SELECT as opening an item/status menu, with form change triggered by using a transformation item from inventory. Alternatively, if the remake intends to simplify the mechanic, document it as an intentional deviation from the original.
